kept.text:
Why Elon doesn't hold on to grudges:

In the year 2000 he was removed from Paypal while on vacation with his wife. His friends had gathered with investors behind his back and staged a coup.

Max Levchin (his co-worker) said:

"He does not hold grudges. He's been remarkably gracious with a bunch of people who basically ousted him as CEO while he was on his honeymoon."

Elon replied:

"I did view the company as at least partly my baby. If I attacked the company and the people there, it's like... it would be attacking my baby. I don't want to do that."

Even when kicked out of his own company, he refused to hold a grudge. 

Later on he would say:

"It might sound corny, but love is the answer."

I think he's right. It makes no sense to hold grudges. Love is the answer in every case.

kept.text: @lemarcaspors_ normalde 10 maç ceza alması gereken adama verilen cezaya bak. Adamlar kaleciyi gözden çıkartsa bile 3 maçtan fazla ceza veremiyorlar. Aha yapı bu işte

在 2000 年代，当马斯克决定同时押注 Tesla 和 SpaceX 时，整个硅谷都觉得他彻底疯了。

不仅是外界，就连彼得·蒂尔这些曾和他一起打下 PayPal 江山的顶级聪明人，都将这视为一场必败的赌局。

彼得·蒂尔当时甚至筹划过一本书，专门给马斯克留了一章，标题极具讽刺意味：一个对风险一无所知的男人。

在他们看来，关于马斯克无视常识的疯狂故事简直多得数不过来。

但商业世界最顶级的降维打击，往往是用结果击碎精英共识。

如果这两家公司只有一家活了下来，旁观者大可轻描淡写地将其归结为幸存者偏差和极致的好运。

但当两个在当时看来完全是异想天开的商业构想，最终双双颠覆了物理世界的运作规律时，哪怕是最顶尖的风投教父也必须重新洗牌自己的认知。

这正是彼得·蒂尔后来的深刻反思：当所谓的奇迹被连续复制，这就绝不仅是运气。

也许根本不是马斯克是个毫无防备的疯子，而是我们所有人都陷入了过度规避风险的平庸陷阱。

在审视风险这盘大棋上，他显然看到了我们根本无法触及的底牌。

quoted_tweet.text:
彼得·蒂尔谈论他会问每一位所投资的初创企业创始人的那个问题：

“既然第20位优秀人才在谷歌能拿到高得多的薪水，工作也不用那么辛苦，而且去谷歌会让他们的简历更好看，那他们为什么还要加入你的公司？”

第20号员工无法获得创始团队成员那样的股权或声誉，因此，如果你想打造一家真正伟大的公司并吸引全球最顶尖的人才，就必须提供其他的激励手段。

蒂尔认为，最好的回答大致是这样的：

“这是全世界唯一一个能让你致力于解决这个极其重要问题的地方。”

“在某种层面上，这必须是一个真正重要的问题，至少要有一部分人认为它是世界上最重要的问题。这类企业才是独一无二的，一旦它们取得成功，最终都将成为各自市场的领军者。”

如果这只是一个其他许多团队都在试图解决的问题，那么你将很难吸引到真正世界级的第20号员工。

kept.text:
First month of my first business, we hired a developer…

…who was rerouting some of our traffic to his own PayPal. Broke everything else he touched too - so it looked like he was just bad (and not a thief)

Took us 6 weeks to figure out why the numbers didn’t add up.

And that’s what got me into recruiting.

I’m Ugis, founder of Recuiter Mill.

Since then: 

> 5 years built and exited an 8-figure online course business
> 5 years headhunting for ecom and SaaS founders
> Work with 8 and 9-figure founder-led companies
> Team of 5 with lists of 150+ vetted companies we source from
> 93% 12-month retention rate (industry average is 70%)
> 3 kids under 3

This is what I post about:

> Why the best candidates aren’t on job boards
> How to reach the best of the best
> What founders get wrong when hiring 
> Real searches: what worked, what didn’t, and why
> Hires that unlocked growth for 8 and 9-figure brands
> What it actually costs not to hire

If you’re a founder wondering what works and what doesn't, stick around.

profile_bio.description: Headhunting for founders: 8 & 9-figure eCom brands, SaaS, coaches, agencies, and of course, @DanBilzerian. Before that, exited an 8-figy online course business.
